summary.title: Local Flask Riddle Game Threat Model

threats.title: No identity verification
threats.description: Any player can choose any username. A user can impersonate another player by entering the same username.
threats.mitigation: Add authentication/session management, or define usernames as non-unique display names only.

threats.title: Username path traversal into local files
threats.description: The username is concatenated into file paths such as data/user-<username>-score.txt and data/user-<username>-guesses.txt. Crafted values could create, overwrite, or access unintended files.
threats.mitigation: Whitelist usernames with ^[A-Za-z0-9_-]{1,30}$, reject dots/path separators, and verify resolved paths remain inside the data directory.

threats.title: No identity verification
threats.description: Any player can choose any username. A user can impersonate another player by entering the same username.
threats.mitigation: Add authentication/session management, or define usernames as non-unique display names only.

threats.title: File creation denial of service
threats.description: Submitting many different or very long usernames can create many files or cause path/IO errors in the data folder.
threats.mitigation: Limit username length and characters, rate-limit submissions where applicable, and handle file IO errors gracefully.

threats.title: Username path traversal into local files
threats.description: The username is concatenated into file paths such as data/user-<username>-score.txt and data/user-<username>-guesses.txt. Crafted values could create, overwrite, or access unintended files.
threats.mitigation: Whitelist usernames with ^[A-Za-z0-9_-]{1,30}$, reject dots/path separators, and verify resolved paths remain inside the data directory.

threats.title: Plain text score manipulation
threats.description: Scores are stored in local text files with predictable names. A local attacker or path-traversal attempt could modify scores or append fake results.
threats.mitigation: Use a database or structured store with server-side validation, validate ownership, and avoid user input directly in filenames.

threats.title: File creation denial of service
threats.description: Submitting many different or very long usernames can create many files or cause path/IO errors in the data folder.
threats.mitigation: Limit username length and characters, rate-limit submissions where applicable, and handle file IO errors gracefully.

threats.title: Tampering with riddle_index
threats.description: The riddle_index comes from the POST form. A user can modify the request to skip riddles, replay riddles, or access an out-of-range index.
threats.mitigation: Store game progress server-side or in a signed session, validate riddle_index is in range, and ignore client-provided progress where possible.

threats.title: Stored or reflected script content in usernames/guesses
threats.description: User-controlled usernames and answers may be written to text files or rendered in templates. If escaping is disabled or bypassed, malicious HTML/JavaScript could execute in the browser.
threats.mitigation: Keep Jinja autoescaping enabled, never mark user input as safe, validate username characters, and escape displayed guesses/highscore entries.

threats.title: Tampering with riddle_index
threats.description: The riddle_index comes from the POST form. A user can modify the request to skip riddles, replay riddles, or access an out-of-range index.
threats.mitigation: Store game progress server-side or in a signed session, validate riddle_index is in range, and ignore client-provided progress where possible.

threats.title: Global username state confusion
threats.description: Attempt counting relies on a global username rather than consistently using the route parameter. Multiple users or requests can cause incorrect attempt counts or score behaviour.
threats.mitigation: Remove global username state and pass username explicitly into functions. Use per-session state where needed.

threats.title: Plain text score manipulation
threats.description: Scores are stored in local text files with predictable names. A local attacker or path-traversal attempt could modify scores or append fake results.
threats.mitigation: Use a database or structured store with server-side validation, validate ownership, and avoid user input directly in filenames.

threats.title: No audit trail for score changes
threats.description: The application does not log key security events such as username creation, failed attempts, riddle completion, or highscore updates.
threats.mitigation: Add minimal server-side logging for important events without logging sensitive payloads.

threats.title: Answer disclosure to template/browser
threats.description: The game process reads the answer file and passes the answers list into the template context. If rendered or exposed through debugging, players can see the answers.
threats.mitigation: Do not pass the full answers list to templates. Keep answer comparison server-side only and disable debug mode outside development.

threats.title: Stored or reflected script content in usernames/guesses
threats.description: User-controlled usernames and answers may be written to text files or rendered in templates. If escaping is disabled or bypassed, malicious HTML/JavaScript could execute in the browser.
threats.mitigation: Keep Jinja autoescaping enabled, never mark user input as safe, validate username characters, and escape displayed guesses/highscore entries.

threats.title: Stored or reflected script content in usernames/guesses
threats.description: User-controlled usernames and answers may be written to text files or rendered in templates. If escaping is disabled or bypassed, malicious HTML/JavaScript could execute in the browser.
threats.mitigation: Keep Jinja autoescaping enabled, never mark user input as safe, validate username characters, and escape displayed guesses/highscore entries.

threats.title: Username path traversal into local files
threats.description: The username is concatenated into file paths such as data/user-<username>-score.txt and data/user-<username>-guesses.txt. Crafted values could create, overwrite, or access unintended files.
threats.mitigation: Whitelist usernames with ^[A-Za-z0-9_-]{1,30}$, reject dots/path separators, and verify resolved paths remain inside the data directory.
